rationale: This is a classic and simple textbook example of the Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag reaction. N-chloro-N-methylbutan-1-amine undergoes intramolecular 1,5-hydrogen atom transfer from the delta-carbon to the nitrogen radical (formed under acidic/light conditions), followed by radical recombination and subsequent base-promoted cyclization to form N-methylpyrrolidine. This example clearly demonstrates the key C-H functionalization at a remote (delta) position and the formation of a five-membered heterocyclic ring, which are the defining features of this reaction.
collision_avoidance_note: The reaction involves an N-haloamine substrate and intramolecular C-H activation to form a pyrrolidine ring. This specific transformation is highly characteristic of the Hofmann-Löffler-Freytag reaction. The acyclic substrate and simple alkyl groups avoid overlap with arene-based reactions (e.g., Sandmeyer, Friedel-Crafts), rearrangements involving carbonyls (e.g., Beckmann, Hofmann rearrangement), or other radical cyclizations that do not start from an N-haloamine.
